channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Thousands flee as Syrian insurgents advance to the doorstep of the country's third-largest city
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Thousands of people fled the central Syrian city of Homs, the country’s third largest, as insurgents seized two towns on the outskirts Friday, positioning themselves for an assault on a potentially major prize in their march against President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court will decide if Palestinian authorities can be sued in US over attacks in Middle East
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court agreed Friday to settle a years-long legal dispute over whether Palestinian authorities can be sued in U.S. courts by Americans killed or wounded in terrorism attacks in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico study's surprising finding: Killer heat hit harder for the young than the elderly
item.description: A surprising study of temperature-related deaths in Mexico upends conventional thinking about what age group is hit hardest by heat.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: United Kingdom cracks down on junk food TV advertisements
item.description: The United Kingdom's Department of Health estimates that 7.2 billion calories will be removed from the diets of British children.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-British soldier pleads not guilty to 'Bloody Sunday' murders after judge clears the way for trial
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A former British soldier will be tried for the murder of two men killed during the 1972 “Bloody Sunday” disturbances in Londonderry, Northern Ireland’s second-biggest city, after a judge refused to dismiss the charges against him.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran launches rocket with heaviest-ever payload into space amid heightened concern over nuclear program
item.description: Iran launches rocket into space carrying its heaviest-ever payload, as security experts remain concerned by its "drastically" increased stockpiles of enriched uranium near nuclear weapons grade purity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Under assault by gangs in Haiti, a Kenyan-led mission fights back
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — An armored vehicle carrying Kenyan police rumbles through Haiti's capital with a shattered window that a high-caliber bullet failed to penetrate, a permanent sign of violence the officers face almost daily in Port-au-Prince.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romanian court throws out presidential results after Russian interference allegations
item.description: Romania’s top court on Friday wiped out results from the first round of presidential elections and called for a rerun, days after declassified Romanian security documents alleged Russian interference that promoted a far-right outsider candidate.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: South Korea's governing party head supports suspending Yoon's powers, making impeachment more likely
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s governing party chief expressed support Friday for suspending the constitutional powers of President Yoon Suk Yeol for imposing martial law this week, in a bombshell reversal that makes Yoon’s impeachment more likely.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Metal detectorist uncovers fragment of Viking sword in the Netherlands
item.description: A 10th-century Viking sword fragment was discovered by a metal detectorist scouring a field in the Netherlands. Further research is being done to find out more about the ancient item.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Lebanon closes all its land border crossings with Syria except one
item.description: Lebanon said it is closing all land border crossings with Syria except for a main one that links Beirut with the Syrian capital Damascus.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Foreign minister says in interview that Russia will use 'all means' to defend its interests
item.description: Russia’s top diplomat says in an interview with former Fox News host Tucker Carlson that Moscow doesn’t want a war with the United States but will use “all means” to defend its interests.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hamas official says Gaza ceasefire talks have resumed after weekslong hiatus
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — A Hamas official said Thursday that international mediators have resumed negotiating with the militant group and Israel over a ceasefire in Gaza, and that he was hopeful a deal to end the 14-month war was within reach.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Notre Dame's reopening will begin with an archbishop's knock on the doors. Here's what comes next
item.description: The reopening this weekend of Notre Dame is a succession of ceremonies to breathe life back into the iconic cathedral and celebrate the recovery from its devastating fire in 2019.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Why the rebel capture of Syria's Hama, a city with a dark history, matters
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — It was one of the darkest moments in the modern history of the Arab world. More than four decades ago, Hafez Assad, then president of Syria, launched what came to be known as the Hama Massacre.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada bans more types of firearms and proposes donating guns to Ukraine
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — Canada said Thursday it is outlawing another 324 firearm varieties — guns the public safety minister said belong on the battlefield, not in the hands of hunters or sport shooters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. raises alarm over Russian interference in Romania’s election
item.description: The Biden administration and lawmakers in Congress are raising alarm over Russian interference in presidential elections in Romania, a NATO ally that borders Ukraine and the Black Sea.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Masked man arrested outside Swedish royal family residence with knife, handcuffs, tape: reports
item.description: A man acting suspiciously while armed outside the home of a Swedish royal family was arrested Wednesday evening, authorities told media outlets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kosovo accuses Serbia of 'terrorist attack' resembling Russian actions in Ukraine
item.description: Kosovo accused Serbia of carrying out a terrorist attack on a water pipeline in the Serb majority north of Kosovo. Serbia denies the accusations, but the recent bloodshed highlights that peace in the Balkans is far from certain.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean president to face impeachment vote this weekend over martial law order, lawmakers say
item.description: South Korean opposition lawmakers will hold a vote to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol after the president declared martial law on Tuesday, citing "anti-state" activities by his opponents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French government collapses in no-confidence vote
item.description: French lawmakers ousted Prime Minister Michel Barnier with a no-confidence vote in the government Wednesday, the first time since 1962.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Lawmakers hold moment of silence for slain Omer Neutra as thousands mourn in hometown synagogue
item.description: A bipartisan group of lawmakers held a moment of silence for the slain Omer Neutra, an American-Israeli who was killed by Hamas on Oct. 7, 2023, according to new intelligence uncovered by the IDF.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico seizes record amount of fentanyl days after Trump issued tariff threats
item.description: Mexican troops seized a record amount of fentanyl pills in the state of Sinaloa on Tuesday, which broke a record, authorities said this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump picks Monica Crowley for senior State Department post
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday nominated Monica Crowley, a former Fox News contributor who faced public scrutiny during Trump’s first term over allegations of plagiarism, for a top public relations post in the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with Hungary’s effort to end Holocaust survivor lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday probed the international consequences of allowing a group of Holocaust survivors and their heirs to leverage U.S. courts to seek compensation from Hungary for confiscating their property.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean parliament votes to defy president by lifting his declaration of martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law late Tuesday, vowing to eliminate “anti-state” forces as he struggles against an opposition that controls the country’s parliament and that he accuses of sympathizing with communist North Korea.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Elton John says he has lost his eyesight, struggles to see new musical
item.description: Elton John says he struggled to watch his new musical because he has lost his eyesight after contracting an infection.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
